D1 Optics L3 Apply Atom

Minimum Deviation Angle

find the geometry where a prism bends light by the smallest possible angle

A prism's total deviation D(θ₁) depends on entry angle. The curve has a single minimum D_m at the symmetric pass — when the ray inside the prism runs parallel to the base.

Sweep θ₁ to trace the deviation curve. The crimson dot tracks the current ray; the gold dashed line marks D_m for the chosen α and n.
